name: pycouchdb

metadata.name: pycouchdb
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# py-couchdb

[![CI](https://github.com/histrio/py-couchdb/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg?branch=no-py2)](https://github.com/histrio/py-couchdb/actions/workflows/main.yml)
![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pycouchdb)
![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/pycouchdb)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/histrio/py-couchdb/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://coveralls.io/github/histrio/py-couchdb?branch=master)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pycouchdb/badge/?version=latest)](https://pycouchdb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)



Modern pure python [CouchDB](https://couchdb.apache.org/) Client.

Currently there are several libraries in python to connect to couchdb. **Why one more?**
It's very simple.

All seems not be maintained, all libraries used standard Python libraries for http requests, and are not compatible with python3.



## Advantages of py-couchdb

- Use [requests](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/latest/) for http requests (much faster than the standard library)
- CouchDB 2.x and CouchDB 3.x compatible
- Also compatible with pypy.


Example:

```python
>>> import pycouchdb
>>> server = pycouchdb.Server("http://admin:admin@localhost:5984/")
>>> server.info()['version']
'1.2.1'
```


## Installation

To install py-couchdb, simply:

```bash
pip install pycouchdb
```

## Documentation

Documentation is available at http://pycouchdb.readthedocs.org.


## Test

To test py-couchdb, simply run:

``` bash
pytest -v --doctest-modules --cov pycouchdb
```

modules.name: pycouchdb.utils
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: pycouchdb.utils.extract_credentials
documentation:
Extract authentication (user name and password) credentials from the
given URL.

```pycon
>>> extract_credentials('http://localhost:5984/_config/')
('http://localhost:5984/_config/', None)
>>> extract_credentials('http://joe:secret@localhost:5984/_config/')
('http://localhost:5984/_config/', ('joe', 'secret'))
>>> extract_credentials('http://joe%40example.com:secret@'
...                      'localhost:5984/_config/')
('http://localhost:5984/_config/', ('joe@example.com', 'secret'))
```

functions.name: pycouchdb.utils.urljoin
documentation:
Assemble a uri based on a base, any number of path segments, and query
string parameters.

```pycon
>>> urljoin('http://example.org', '_all_dbs')
'http://example.org/_all_dbs'
```

A trailing slash on the uri base is handled gracefully:

```pycon
>>> urljoin('http://example.org/', '_all_dbs')
'http://example.org/_all_dbs'
```

And multiple positional arguments become path parts:

```pycon
>>> urljoin('http://example.org/', 'foo', 'bar')
'http://example.org/foo/bar'
```

```pycon
>>> urljoin('http://example.org/', 'foo/bar')
'http://example.org/foo/bar'
```

```pycon
>>> urljoin('http://example.org/', 'foo', '/bar/')
'http://example.org/foo/bar/'
```

```pycon
>>> urljoin('http://example.com', 'org.couchdb.user:username')
'http://example.com/org.couchdb.user:username'
```

functions.name: pycouchdb.utils._path_from_name
documentation:
Expand a 'design/foo' style name to its full path as a list of
segments.

```pycon
>>> _path_from_name("_design/test", '_view')
['_design', 'test']
>>> _path_from_name("design/test", '_view')
['_design', 'design', '_view', 'test']
```

functions.name: pycouchdb.utils.encode_view_options
documentation:
Encode any items in the options dict that are sent as a JSON string to a
view/list function.

```pycon
>>> opts = {'key': 'foo', "notkey":"bar"}
>>> res = encode_view_options(opts)
>>> res["key"], res["notkey"]
('"foo"', 'bar')
```

```pycon
>>> opts = {'startkey': 'foo', "endkey":"bar"}
>>> res = encode_view_options(opts)
>>> res['startkey'], res['endkey']
('"foo"', '"bar"')
```

classes.name: pycouchdb.feedreader.BaseFeedReader

methods.name: pycouchdb.feedreader.BaseFeedReader.on_message
documentation:
Callback method that is called when change
message is received from couchdb.

```
param message

change object

returns

None
```

methods.name: pycouchdb.feedreader.BaseFeedReader.on_close
documentation:
Callback method that is received when connection
is closed with a server. By default, does nothing.

methods.name: pycouchdb.feedreader.BaseFeedReader.on_heartbeat
documentation:
Callback method invoked when a hearbeat (empty line) is received
from the _changes stream. Override this to purge the reader's internal
buffers (if any) if it waited too long without receiving anything.
Base interface class for changes feed reader.

classes.name: pycouchdb.feedreader.SimpleFeedReader
documentation:
Simple feed reader that encapsule any callable in
a valid feed reader interface.

% versionadded: 1.10

Class that represents a couchdb connection.

```
param verify

setup ssl verification.

param base_url

a full url to couchdb (can contain auth data).

param full_commit

If False, couchdb not commits all data on a
request is finished.

param authmethod

specify a authentication method. By default "basic"
method is used but also exists "session" (that requires
some server configuration changes).
```

% versionchanged: 1.4
% Set basic auth method as default instead of session method.

% versionchanged: 1.5
% Add verify parameter for setup ssl verificaton

methods.name: pycouchdb.client.Database.compact

Send compact message to server. Compacting write-heavy databases
should be avoided, otherwise the process may not catch up with
the writes. Read load has no effect.
